feature_name: v1.22.2 three-FR polish round (all SMALL) after Dean's on-device iPhone pass of v1.22.1 (everything working). FR-1 AUDIO FULLSCREEN: a working 'fullscreen' (CSS-expanded now-playing) view for AUDIO playback -- v1.22.1 FR-2 REMOVED the mobile-audio #fs-btn because enterFullscreen() -> webkitEnterFullscreen() is a no-op for an audio-only element on iOS; iPhone Safari does NOT support the Fullscreen API on non-video elements, so the fix must be a CSS full-viewport 'expanded now-playing' view (position:fixed/inset:0, high z-index, class-toggle) enlarging #audio-bg-art + the custom control bar -- NOT the Fullscreen API. Toggleable (enter+exit), scoped to audio-mode, must NOT regress video fullscreen (stays on the real Fullscreen API/webkitEnterFullscreen). Dean's final word: 'if we add the fullscreen button i just want it to work' -- built to actually work, his iPhone on-device pass is the arbiter. FR-2 FAVICON: displays inconsistently -- shows in a fresh tab but NOT as a bookmark icon / on certain browsers. FR-3 STAR-RATING WRAP: the 'N / 5' rating text is compressed and wraps to '3 /' on one line + '5' underneath.
branch: feature/v1.22.2-audio-fullscreen (suggested; off main at v1.22.1). Coordinator owns ALL git.
base: main (v1.22.1). Prior shipped record: docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-07-v1.22.1-mobile-player-fixes.md (the round that REMOVED the mobile-audio #fs-btn this round re-introduces as an audio-expand affordance).
scope_summary: THREE SMALL FRs: FR-1 audio 'fullscreen' (CSS-expanded now-playing) view; FR-2 favicon consistency (tab shows but bookmark/some browsers do not); FR-3 star-rating 'N / 5' one-line (stop the wrap). Resist scope creep beyond these three. EXPLICIT NON-ASK: audio press-and-hold-2x is deliberately OUT of scope -- Dean is OK with it not working since the persistent #speed-btn covers speed; do NOT touch hold-2x for audio or add any work for it, leave it exactly as shipped in v1.22.1. Coordinator owns ALL git; SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint output and DO NOT commit. Node 22 toolchain at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-coder-projects-filetube/139c0e56-b545-4e8e-ba05-f892f6dd6d0d/scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin (prepend to PATH). Tests npm test, lint npm run lint (baseline 0 errors, 8 pre-existing common.js warnings). No headless/E2E -- Dean's iPhone on-device pass is the documented arbiter for the fullscreen feel (docs/RELIABILITY.md).

grounded_diagnosis.fr1_audio_fullscreen: Grounded directly in the live v1.22.1 code. The mobile-audio #fs-btn is currently HIDDEN via CSS (style.css:3226-3233: `#player-wrapper.ff-mobile.audio-mode #fs-btn { display: none; }`), a v1.22.1 FR-2 decision because enterFullscreen() is a no-op for audio on iOS. The #fs-btn click handler (player.js:1707-1721) unconditionally routes through enterFullscreen()/inNativeFullscreen() -> native Fullscreen API. enterFullscreen() (player.js:1500-1511): iOS -> mediaPlayer.webkitEnterFullscreen() (returns null; no-op for audio -- no video surface); desktop -> host.requestFullscreen() (host = .player-container). For AUDIO, .audio-mode is set on #player-wrapper; the cover art is #audio-bg-art (style.css:909-924: position:absolute; inset:0; z-index:0; shown+pointer-events:auto only in .audio-mode). The custom control bar .player-controls is z-index 8 (style.css:2903-2912). FIX SHAPE (per Dean + PE to finalize): a CSS full-viewport expanded now-playing view driven by a class toggle (position:fixed; inset:0; high z-index) that enlarges #audio-bg-art + the custom bar; NOT the Fullscreen API. RECOMMENDED (PE's call): reuse the existing #fs-btn -- re-show it for mobile audio (undo/scope the 3226-3233 hide) and add an audio-mode branch to the #fs-btn click handler that toggles the CSS-expand class INSTEAD of calling enterFullscreen() when in .audio-mode -- avoiding new four-shell #player-host-template markup churn. Video fullscreen path (enterFullscreen/webkitEnterFullscreen/inNativeFullscreen) stays byte-identical.
key_design_questions_for_pe: Reuse #fs-btn for audio (re-show + audio-mode branch to the CSS-expand path) vs a distinct new control? Recommend reusing #fs-btn to avoid four-shell markup churn -- PE's final call. If a new control id IS added, it MUST be byte-identical across all four #player-host-template shells (index.html, setup.html, watch.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html), same #pip-btn/#speed-btn parity-test discipline.

The CSS-expand overlay must COEXIST with: the persistent player's dock FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED model, the #audio-bg-art layer + .art-play-glyph, Media Session, progress saving, loop, and the existing v1.21 THEATRE mode (style.css:3920+, which lives on .watch-container -- a page-level layout, separate from #player-wrapper -- confirm NO conflict). Must not fight the persistent single <video> / one-time wireHostListeners model.

Exit affordance: a close/exit button and/or tapping and/or the same #fs-btn toggling back. Must be discoverable AND restore the prior layout cleanly (no stuck-expanded state across dock transitions / navigation / close).
Scope: FULL-only (like other fullscreen behavior), or also DOCKED? Likely FULL-only -- PE confirms.
Persist nothing server-side. If any state is needed it is ephemeral/local (in-memory or localStorage), never on the server.
Interaction with the dock model: if the player docks or closes (CLOSED) while expanded, the expand class must be cleared so a re-open never starts stuck-expanded.
grounded_diagnosis.explicit_non_ask: Audio press-and-hold-2x: Dean explicitly said he is OK with it not working since the persistent #speed-btn covers speed. DO NOT touch hold-2x for audio or add any work for it. Leave it exactly as shipped in v1.22.1.
grounded_diagnosis.fr2_favicon: Dean verbatim: 'on certain browsers the favicon doesn't display, can we make it display consistently? it does in a new tab section but not if a bookmark?' Grounded in the live shells: all FOUR shells (public/index.html, public/watch.html, public/setup.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html) carry BYTE-IDENTICAL icon links (lines 7-9): `<link rel=icon type=image/svg+xml href=/favicon.svg>`, `<link rel=apple-touch-icon href=/icons/icon-192.png>`, `<link rel=manifest href=/manifest.webmanifest>`. Assets present in public/: favicon.svg, icons/icon-192.png, icons/icon-512.png, manifest.webmanifest -- there is NO favicon.ico and NO PNG `rel=icon` fallback. LEADING CAUSE (PM/PE to confirm): the ONLY tab favicon is SVG-only; several browsers + most bookmark bars/history/OS surfaces do NOT use an SVG favicon and fall back to a default when no ICO/PNG `rel=icon` (with explicit sizes/type) exists -- exactly Dean's 'shows in a fresh tab but not as a bookmark / inconsistent across browsers' symptom. LIKELY FIX SHAPE (PE finalizes): add a raster fallback -- a favicon.ico and/or PNG `rel=icon` links with explicit sizes/type -- across all four shells (byte-identical, four-shell parity discipline), reusing/deriving from the existing icon-192/512 assets rather than adding a new design; verify the /favicon.svg static route + cache headers do not serve a stale/404 icon. NO new runtime deps; keep it small/low-risk. LIGHT gate.
grounded_diagnosis.fr3_star_rating_wrap: Dean verbatim: 'the x/5 star thing is now compressed so it looks like 3/ and then on a line under, the 5.' Grounded: the watch-page rating text element #rating-text has class .rating-count (style.css:1365-1369: font-size 11px; margin-left 6px -- NO white-space/flex-shrink), sits inside .star-rating (public/watch.html:166-172; style.css:1349-1353: display:flex; align-items:center; gap:2px), and watch.js:779 sets its textContent to `${rating} / 5` (e.g. '3 / 5'). Under a width squeeze in the .watch-actions row the flex item wraps its 'N / 5' text to 'N /' + '5' on the next line -- exactly Dean's symptom. TRIVIAL CSS FIX (PE/SDE finalize): white-space:nowrap on .rating-count (and/or flex-shrink:0 so the count is not squeezed), keeping era tokens. Confirm the home-card variant (.card-rating, main.js:468, all stars glyphs not 'N / 5' text) is NOT affected -- the wrap is the watch-page text count. LIGHT gate.

hard_constraints: MINIMAL, surgical scope: exactly THREE small FRs (FR-1 audio CSS-expanded fullscreen; FR-2 favicon consistency; FR-3 star-rating one-line). NO rework of the player/shell/CSS architecture beyond what each FR requires. Resist scope creep.

FR-2/FR-3 are trivial and independent of the player: any icon-link change (FR-2) MUST be byte-identical across all four shells (index.html, watch.html, setup.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html) per the four-shell parity discipline; the star-rating fix (FR-3) is CSS-only against .rating-count/.star-rating and must not affect the home-card .card-rating glyph variant.
Do NOT use the browser Fullscreen API for audio (iPhone Safari refuses it on non-video elements). MUST be a CSS full-viewport class-toggle overlay (position:fixed/inset:0/high z-index).

Do NOT regress VIDEO fullscreen: the video path (enterFullscreen -> webkitEnterFullscreen on iOS / host.requestFullscreen on desktop, inNativeFullscreen, the fs-btn video branch) stays byte-identical.
Do NOT touch audio press-and-hold-2x (explicit Dean non-ask). Leave hold-2x exactly as shipped in v1.22.1.

Additive / no regressions: preserve every v1.16/v1.21/v1.22.0/v1.22.1 player feature -- persistent single <video id=media-player>, one-time wireHostListeners(), dock FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED, prev/next, autoplay-next, transcode+resume overlays, Media Session, progress saving, #audio-bg-art, loop toggle, cross-tab, PiP, THEATRE mode, the v1.22.1 custom-bar-everywhere + #speed-btn + FR-5 desktop click-to-pause.

If reusing #fs-btn: re-showing it for mobile audio must NOT resurrect the dead-tap bug -- the audio branch must route to the CSS-expand path, never enterFullscreen(). If a NEW control id is added instead, it MUST be byte-identical across all four #player-host-template shells (parity-test, #pip-btn/#speed-btn discipline).

Preserve the era-theme token system for all new/changed CSS (the expand overlay, the exit/toggle affordance, enlarged art + bar) -- no hardcoded colors bypassing tokens; >=44px touch targets on mobile per the existing convention.

Reuse the SINGLE shared mobile-detection helper (resolveMobileFormFactor/isMobileFormFactor) -- never a second divergent 'is this mobile' signal. (The CSS-expand should work everywhere per Dean, but any JS gating must reuse the shared signal.)

Follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes, textContent over innerHTML, node:test for every new pure helper + a regression lock, lint 0 warnings, NO new runtime dependencies.
Node.js 22 LTS toolchain; no headless-browser/E2E infra (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- Dean's iPhone on-device pass is the documented arbiter for the audio-fullscreen feel.
review_tier_proposed: Per Dean's minimal directive: gate PER FR, not one blanket tier. FR-1 (audio fullscreen) touches the persistent player (the expand overlay must coexist cleanly with the dock FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED model + #audio-bg-art + Media Session + progress saving + THEATRE mode), so a FOCUSED two-reviewer pass on the overlay/dock interaction (stuck-expanded state across dock/close/navigation; video-fullscreen non-regression) is warranted -- but this is NOT the FR-1-scale critical path of v1.22.1. FR-2 (favicon, static shells/assets) and FR-3 (star-rating CSS) are trivial/low-risk -> LIGHT single-QA + the four-shell parity check for FR-2's icon links. Dean's iPhone on-device pass is the ultimate arbiter for the audio-fullscreen feel and the favicon/bookmark rendering across his browsers.
dean_speed_directive: SMALL round, three FRs. Prefer the smallest correct fix over any refactor. Anticipated task shape (EM finalizes after PE Design): FR-1 -> T1 (public/js/player.js -- the #fs-btn audio-mode branch / expand-toggle logic + any pure helper/tests) || T2 (public/css/style.css -- the .audio-mode expand overlay class, enlarged #audio-bg-art + custom bar, exit affordance, era tokens, touch targets), disjoint files, mirrors the v1.22/v1.22.1 T1||T2 split with a frozen contract; a four-shell touch folded in ONLY IF a new control id is added (PE prefers reusing #fs-btn to avoid it). FR-2 (favicon) -> a small static task touching the four shells (byte-identical icon links) + any raster asset. FR-3 (star-rating) -> trivial CSS, foldable into the FR-1 T2 style.css task or a tiny standalone task. PE/EM decide whether FR-2/FR-3 ride the same wave (they touch different files -- shells/assets vs style.css/player.js -- so can parallelize cleanly).

history.note: ENGINEERING-MANAGER opened v1.22.2 'audio fullscreen' -- a TINY one-FR follow-up after Dean's on-device iPhone pass of v1.22.1 (everything working; one remaining ask + one explicit non-ask). Grounded the diagnosis directly in the live v1.22.1 code before routing: read the #fs-btn click handler (player.js:1707-1721, unconditionally routes through enterFullscreen()/inNativeFullscreen()), enterFullscreen() (player.js:1500-1511, iOS webkitEnterFullscreen -> null/no-op for audio; desktop host.requestFullscreen()), the #audio-bg-art cover-art layer + .audio-mode (player.js:1770-1824, 1937-1985; style.css:909-931), the custom control bar (.player-controls z-index 8, style.css:2903-2912), the v1.22.1 mobile-audio #fs-btn HIDE (style.css:3226-3233 -- the exact rule that removed the dead-tap fs-btn this round re-introduces as a CSS-expand affordance), and confirmed the v1.21 THEATRE mode lives on .watch-container (style.css:3920+), a page-level layout separate from #player-wrapper's dock model. Confirmed the fix must be a CSS full-viewport class-toggle overlay (position:fixed/inset:0/high z-index) enlarging #audio-bg-art + the custom bar -- NOT the Fullscreen API (iPhone Safari refuses it on non-video elements). Recommended (PE to finalize) reusing #fs-btn with an .audio-mode branch to avoid four-shell markup churn, keeping the video-fullscreen path byte-identical. Captured Dean's EXPLICIT NON-ASK: do NOT touch audio hold-2x. No application/test code written; no git touched.

ENGINEERING-MANAGER re-scoped mid-bootstrap: coordinator relayed two more SMALL Dean asks, making v1.22.2 a THREE-FR round. FR-2 FAVICON (grounded live): all four shells carry byte-identical icon links but the only tab favicon is SVG-only (`rel=icon type=image/svg+xml href=/favicon.svg`) with NO favicon.ico / PNG `rel=icon` fallback -- the classic 'shows in a fresh tab but not as a bookmark / inconsistent across browsers' symptom; likely fix = add a raster (.ico/PNG with explicit sizes/type) fallback across all four shells (parity), derived from the existing icon-192/512 assets. FR-3 STAR-RATING WRAP (grounded live): #rating-text (.rating-count, style.css:1365-1369, no white-space/flex-shrink) inside .star-rating flex (watch.html:166-172) gets textContent 'N / 5' (watch.js:779) and wraps to 'N /' + '5' under a width squeeze; trivial CSS fix (white-space:nowrap / flex-shrink:0), not affecting the home-card .card-rating glyph variant. Updated feature_name/scope_summary/grounded_diagnosis (added fr2_favicon + fr3_star_rating_wrap)/hard_constraints/review_tier_proposed/dean_speed_directive to three FRs. Gate per-FR: FR-1 focused two-reviewer (persistent-player overlay/dock), FR-2/FR-3 light single-QA + four-shell parity for FR-2. Set stage=discovery and wrote the product-manager Discovery inbox with all three grounded FRs + Dean's minimal-scope directive + the FR-1 key design questions for PE + the audio hold-2x non-ask. No application/test code written; no git touched. Routing to product-manager for Discovery; on completion, coordinator runs /prep-pe-design.

PRINCIPAL-ENGINEER completed Design. Read all three FRs against live v1.22.1 code before designing: player.js (enterFullscreen ~1500-1511, #fs-btn handler ~1707-1721, pure-helpers block + module.exports ~290-416, state constants + element refs ~421-613, wireHostListeners keydown/orientation ~1826-1905, teardownMediaState ~1909-1969 with the host.classList.remove('audio-mode') at 1937, setupForMedia audio-mode add at 1985, dock() ~2069, close() ~2083); style.css (.audio-bg-art ~909-931, .player-controls z-index 8 ~2907, .fs-btn ~3046, the v1.22.1 mobile-audio #fs-btn hide ~3226-3233, .star-rating/.rating-count ~1348-1369, THEATRE mode on .watch-container ~3939, max z-index tiers header 1000/dock 950/nav 900/modals 2000-2250); all four shells' byte-identical icon <link>s (lines 7-9, no favicon.ico/PNG rel=icon present); server.js express.static(public, no-cache) ~1789 confirming /icons/*.png resolves with no route change; watch.js:779; main.js:468; and the existing parity/asset test patterns (player-pip-parity.test.js, pwa-icons.test.js). DECISIONS: FR-1 mechanism = REUSE #fs-btn with an .audio-mode branch (new pure helper resolveFsButtonAction({audioMode}) -> 'audio-expand' | 'native-fullscreen', exported + unit-tested; toggleAudioExpand()/exitAudioExpand() flipping a #player-wrapper.audio-expanded class), FULL-only, exit by re-tapping the same button (primary) + a dedicated desktop Esc listener (secondary), force-cleared in teardownMediaState/dock/close so it never sticks (AC5); the native video Fullscreen block is copied byte-identical inside the new branch (AC4); NO new control id, so zero four-shell markup churn for FR-1. FR-2 format = PNG rel=icon fallbacks (type/sizes) reusing the committed /icons/icon-192.png + icon-512.png -- NO .ico, no asset-generation, no dependency -- inserted byte-identically across the four shells; SVG/apple-touch-icon/manifest unchanged. FR-3 = white-space:nowrap + flex-shrink:0 on .rating-count only (home-card .card-rating unaffected). TASK/WAVE PLAN (single wave, no shared files): T1 public/js/player.js + test/unit/player-audio-expand.test.js (FR-1 JS) || T2 public/css/style.css (FR-1 overlay + re-show mobile-audio #fs-btn + FR-3) || T3 the four shells' icon <link>s + extend test/unit/pwa-icons.test.js (FR-2). Frozen T1<->T2 contract: class name audio-expanded on #player-wrapper; #fs-btn toggles it in .audio-mode. Wrote the ## Design section (approach, per-FR mechanics, file-overlap map/task plan, alternatives, risks, perf) into the exec plan; added a Progress-log entry; set artifacts.design + stage=design-complete-ready-for-em-task-breakdown. Markdownlint-clean. No application/test code written; no git touched. Ready for EM task breakdown.
